user_query:
I need you to update a markdown TODO list with several changes and then produce a structured report. Read these files under input/:
- input/TODO_seed.md — the starting TODO list
- input/instructions.md — the exact operations to apply
- input/today.json — a canonical reference date for overdue calculations in the report

Apply all changes described in input/instructions.md to the TODO list in input/TODO_seed.md. Only modify TODO list lines; keep all non-TODO content (like headings, blank lines, and notes) intact. Preserve the standard checkbox syntax (- [ ] for open, - [x] for done) and inline markers (#tags, !priority, @due(YYYY-MM-DD)).

After applying the changes:
1) Write the fully updated TODO file to output/TODO.md.
2) Write a JSON report to output/report.json with this exact structure:
{
  "today": "<value from input/today.json>",
  "open_count": <int>,
  "done_count": <int>,
  "open": [
    { "text": "<full todo line text without the '- [ ]' prefix>", "tags": ["..."], "priority": "high|medium|low|null", "dueDate": "YYYY-MM-DD|null" }
  ],
  "overdue_open": [
    { "text": "...", "tags": ["..."], "priority": "...", "dueDate": "..." }
  ],
  "tag_counts": { "<tag>": <count>, ... },
  "priority_counts": { "high": <int>, "medium": <int>, "low": <int> },
  "search": {
    "high_priority_dev_overdue": ["<texts of open items that contain #dev, have !high, and have @due earlier than 'today'>"]
  }
}

Rules for the report:
- The open and done counts must be consistent with output/TODO.md after all modifications.
- The "open" array must list all open TODOs as objects and include their extracted tags (lowercased, deduplicated), priority (lowercased or null), and dueDate (YYYY-MM-DD or null). The "text" field should include the full todo content without the checkbox prefix but with any inline markers preserved.
- The "overdue_open" array must include only open items whose dueDate is strictly before the date in input/today.json. Sort this array by dueDate ascending. If an item has no dueDate, it is not considered overdue.
- "tag_counts" should aggregate counts across open items only (lowercased tags). "priority_counts" should count open items by priority (treat missing priority as not counted in any bucket).
- The "search.high_priority_dev_overdue" array should include the "text" values (as defined above) of open items that have both the #dev tag and !high priority and are overdue relative to the provided today.

Important implementation details:
- Use the header-aware insertion described in input/instructions.md when adding new items: if a specified header exists, insert under that header skipping immediate blank lines; if it does not exist, append after the last existing TODO item.
- When editing a TODO, preserve its done/open state and all markers not explicitly changed by the instruction.
- When marking done, only change the checkbox state for that specific item.
- When removing, delete the entire line for that TODO.

Write the results to exactly:
- output/TODO.md
- output/report.json
